title: Supreme Court Rules Against Trump's Tariffs: These 10 Stocks Are Soaring
author: Piero Cingari
description: The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 that President Trump's sweeping global tariffs were unconstitutional, finding that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act does not authorize the president to impose tariffs without clear congressional authorization. The decision triggered a sharp rally across U.S. equities, with 10 stocks posting significant gains following the ruling that invalidates tariffs that had reached up to 145% on some products.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 13.2% following the tariff ruling, benefiting from removal of duties that had impacted import costs and trade flows.
Listed among the 10 stocks but declined 3.37%, suggesting the tariff ruling had minimal positive impact or other factors offset potential gains.
Stock gained 7.66% as the removal of tariffs reduces operational costs and improves market conditions for tech/internet infrastructure companies.
Despite being listed among the 10 stocks, it declined 46.1%, indicating the tariff ruling was overshadowed by other negative company-specific factors.
Stock rose 5.43% as tariff removal reduces costs for publishing and educational materials that rely on imported components.
Stock surged 36.4%, significantly benefiting from tariff elimination which reduces costs for communications technology and hardware.
Stock gained 9.37% as removal of tariffs reduces costs for solar equipment and components, improving margins for renewable energy companies.
Stock gained 3.44% as tariff removal reduces costs for power semiconductor and component manufacturing.
Stock rose 7.56% as the tariff ruling benefits industrial and manufacturing companies that rely on imported materials and components.

title: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures  Slip After Weaker-Than-Expected GDP Report—Grail, Candel Therapeutics, Copart In Focus (UPDATED)
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures rose on Friday ahead of Q4 GDP data and PCE inflation gauge releases. Markets are pricing in a 94% likelihood of unchanged Fed rates in March. Notable movers include Copart missing earnings expectations, Comfort Systems raising dividends, Grail plunging 47% on weak earnings, Candel Therapeutics declining on stock offering announcement, and Dropbox falling despite beating earnings. Wells Fargo strategist recommends leaning into growth-sensitive sectors like Financials and Industrials rather than defensive positioning.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock dropped 6.64% after missing Q2 earnings estimates (36 cents vs. 40 cents consensus). Maintains weak price trend across all timeframes.
Stock rose 4.57% after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results and raising quarterly dividend. Maintains stronger price trend across short, medium, and long terms.
Stock plunged 47.04% following Q4 earnings with $41.3M revenue and $99.2M net loss. Maintains weaker price trend across all timeframes.
Stock declined 12.27% after announcing $100 million public stock offering. Maintains weak price trend across all timeframes.
Stock fell 4.16% despite beating Q4 earnings expectations, with FY2026 guidance below market estimates. Maintains weaker price trend across all timeframes.

title: Copart Stock Drops After Q2 Earnings: Here's Why
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Copart (NASDAQ: CPRT) stock dropped significantly in after-hours trading following Q2 earnings that missed analyst expectations. The company reported EPS of 36 cents versus the consensus estimate of 40 cents, and quarterly revenue of $1.12 billion fell short of the $1.14 billion estimate and declined year-over-year from $1.16 billion. Shares fell 11.16% in after-hours trading.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Copart missed both earnings per share (36¢ vs. 40¢ expected) and revenue ($1.12B vs. $1.14B expected) estimates, with revenue declining year-over-year. The stock fell 11.16% in after-hours trading, reflecting investor disappointment with the financial results.

title: Weybosset Research & Management Waves Goodbye to $3.3 Million Worth of MaxLinear Shares
author: Jake Lerch
description: Weybosset Research & Management LLC fully exited its position in MaxLinear on January 6, 2026, selling all 205,893 shares worth approximately $3.31 million. The exit reflects MaxLinear's poor performance, with stock down 42% from early 2023 and revenue declining 62% over three years, while the company has shifted from $101 million in net income in 2022 to a $180 million net loss.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Major institutional investor fully exited position due to deteriorating fundamentals including 42% stock decline over 3 years, 62% revenue drop, and shift from $101M profit to $180M net loss. Stock underperformed S&P 500 by 25 percentage points.
